name: pijul
description: A distributed version control system that is at the same time theoretically sound, fast and easy to learn and use

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Adds a path to the tree

options.description: Print help information

args.name: PATHS
args.description: Paths to add to the internal tree

subcommands.name: apply
subcommands.description: Applies changes to a channel

options.description: Apply change to this channel
Only apply the dependencies of the change, not the change itself. Only applicable for a single change
Print help information
Set the repository where this command should run. Defaults to the first ancestor of the current directory that contains a .pijul directory

args.name: CHANGE
args.description: The change that need to be applied. If this value is missing, read the change in text format on the standard input

subcommands.name: archive
subcommands.description: Creates an archive of the repository

options.description: Apply these changes after switching to the channel
Use this channel, instead of the current channel
Print help information
Do not check certificates (HTTPS remotes only, this option might be dangerous)
Name of the output file
Append this path in front of each path inside the archive
Ask the remote to send an archive
Set the repository where this command should run. Defaults to the first ancestor of the current directory that contains a .pijul directory
Archive in this state
Append this path in front of each path inside the archive

subcommands.name: change
subcommands.description: Shows information about a particular change

options.description: Print help information
Use the repository at PATH instead of the current directory

args.name: HASH
args.description: The hash of the change to show, or an unambiguous prefix thereof

subcommands.name: channel
subcommands.description: Manages different channels

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Delete a channel. The channel must not be the current channel

options.description: Print help information

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

subcommands.name: new
subcommands.description: Create a new, empty channel

subcommands.name: rename
subcommands.description: Rename a channel

options.description: Print help information

subcommands.name: switch
subcommands.description: Switch to a channel. There must not be unrecorded changes in the working copy

options.description: Print help information
Set the repository where this command should run. Defaults to the first ancestor of the current directory that contains a .pijul directory

subcommands.name: clone
subcommands.description: Clones an existing pijul repository

options.description: Clone this change and its dependencies
Set the remote channel [default: main]
Print help information
Do not check certificates (HTTPS remotes only, this option might be dangerous)
Clone this path only
Clone this state

args.name: PATH
args.description: Path where to clone the repository. If missing, the inferred name of the remote repository is used

subcommands.name: credit
subcommands.description: Shows which change last affected each line of the given file(s)

options.description: Use this channel instead of the current channel
Print help information
Set the repository where this command should run. Defaults to the first ancestor of the current directory that contains a .pijul directory

subcommands.name: diff
subcommands.description: Shows difference between two channels/changes

options.description: Compare with this channel
Print help information
Output the diff in JSON format instead of the default change text format
Set the repository where this command should run. Defaults to the first ancestor of the current directory that contains a .pijul directory
Show a short version of the diff
Add all the changes of this channel as dependencies (except changes implied transitively), instead of the minimal dependencies
Include the untracked files

args.name: PREFIXES
args.description: Only diff those paths (files or directories). If missing, diff the entire repository

subcommands.name: fork
subcommands.description: Create a new channel

options.description: Apply this change after creating the channel
Make the new channel from this channel instead of the current channel
Print help information
Set the repository where this command should run. Defaults to the first ancestor of the current directory that contains a .pijul directory

args.name: TO
args.description: The name of the new channel

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initializes an empty pijul repository

options.description: Set the name of the current channel (defaults to “main”)
Print help information
Project kind; if Pijul knows about your project kind, the .ignore file will be populated with a conservative list of commonly ignored entries. Example: pijul init --kind=rust

args.name: PATH
args.description: Path where the repository should be initialized

subcommands.name: log
subcommands.description: Show the entire log of changes

options.description: Show logs for this channel instead of the current channel
Include full change description in the output
Print help information
Only show the change hashes
Output at most this many changes
Start after this many changes
Set the repository where this command should run. Defaults to the first ancestor of the current directory that contains a .pijul directory
Include state identifiers in the output

args.name: FILTERS
args.description: Filter log output, showing only log entries that touched the specified files. Accepted as a list of paths relative to your current directory. Currently, filters can only be applied when logging the ch

subcommands.name: pull
subcommands.description: Pulls changes from a remote upstream

options.description: Pull all changes
Force an update of the local remote cache. May effect some reporting of unrecords/concurrent changes in the remote
Pull from this remote channel
Download full changes, even when not necessary
Print help information
Do not check certificates (HTTPS remotes only, this option might be dangerous)
Only pull to these paths
Set the repository where this command should run. Defaults to the first ancestor of the current directory that contains a .pijul directory
Pull into this channel instead of the current channel

args.name: CHANGES
args.description: Pull changes from the local repository, not necessarily from a channel

subcommands.name: push
subcommands.description: Pushes changes to a remote upstream

options.description: Push all changes
Force an update of the local remote cache. May effect some reporting of unrecords/concurrent changes in the remote
Push from this channel instead of the default channel
Print help information
Do not check certificates (HTTPS remotes only, this option might be dangerous)
Push changes only relating to these paths
Path to the repository. Uses the current repository if the argument is omitted
Push to this remote channel instead of the remote’s default channel

subcommands.name: record
subcommands.description: Creates a new change

options.description: Record all paths that have changed
Amend this change instead of creating a new change
Set the author field
Record the change in this channel instead of the current channel
Print help information
Ignore missing (deleted) files
Set the change message
Set the repository where this command should run. Defaults to the first ancestor of the current directory that contains a .pijul directory
Set the timestamp field

args.name: PREFIXES
args.description: Paths in which to record the changes

subcommands.name: remote
subcommands.description: Manages remote repositories

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Deletes the remote

options.description: Print help information

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

options.description: Print help information
Set the repository where this command should run. Defaults to the first ancestor of the current directory that contains a .pijul directory

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Removes a file from the tree of tracked files (pijul record will then record this as a deletion)

options.description: Print help information

args.name: PATHS
args.description: The paths need to be removed

subcommands.name: reset
subcommands.description:
Resets the working copy to the last recorded change.
In other words, discards all unrecorded changes

options.description: Reset the working copy to this channel, and change the current channel to this channel
Print this file to the standard output, without modifying the repository (works for a single file only)
Reset even if there are unrecorded changes
Print help information
Set the repository where this command should run. Defaults to the first ancestor of the current directory that contains a .pijul directory

subcommands.name: unrecord
subcommands.description: Unrecords a list of changes

options.description: Unrecord changes from this channel instead of the current channel
Print help information
Set the repository where this command should run. Defaults to the first ancestor of the current directory that contains a .pijul directory
Also undo the changes in the working copy (preserving unrecorded changes if there are any)
Show N changes in a text editor if no s were given. Defaults to the value of unrecord_changes in your global configuration

args.name: CHANGE_ID
args.description: The hash of a change (unambiguous prefixes are accepted)
